LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT INFINITY INSURANCE COMPANY, A INDIANA CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. OFF CALENDAR. A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS FILED FEBRUARY 23, 2012. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077
DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT ; REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE FILED BY DEFENDANT INFINITY INSURANCE COMPANY, A INDIANA CORPORATION HEARING SET FOR MAY-29-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER FILED BY DEFENDANT INFINITY INSURANCE COMPANY, A INDIANA CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; DEFENDANT INFINITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ALL RESPECT EXCEPT THE PLAINTIFF MUST IDENTIFY THE NAME OF SALESPERSON WHO MADE THE MISREPRESENTATION OR AFTER REASONABLE EFFORT, PLAINTIFF CANNOT DETERMINE HIS NAME. THE COURT GRANTED 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT. THE FRAUD CAUSES OF ACTION ARE PLED WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY. THE SPECIFICITY REQUIREMENT IS RELAXED WHERE THE ALLEGATIONS SHOW THAT THE DEFENDANT LIKELY POSSESSES FULL INFORMATION CONCERNING THE FACTS OF THE CONTROVERSY. PARAGRAPH 43 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT THE SALESPERSON WAS DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEE AND HE CAN BE IDENTIFIED BY LOOKING AT EMPLOYEE RECORDS. PLAINTIFF PLEADS JUSTIFIABLE RELIANCE AND THE FRAUD EXCEPTION TO THE PAROLE EVIDENCE RULE MAY APPLY. THE SALESPERSON TRANSLATED THE DOCUMENTS FOR PLAINTIFF AND MISREPRESENTED THE TERMS. PLAINITFF'S ALLEGATIONS OF CONCEALMENT ARE PROPER EVEN THOUGH A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP DOES NOT EXIST. CONCEALMENT CAN BE ESTABLISHED WHERE THERE IS PARTIAL DISCLOSURE. REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER BY WAY OF E-MAIL) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-29-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF AUG-29-2012 IS OFF CALENDAR - DISMISSAL ON FILE. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-15-2012 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-18-2012 Continued To Jul-18-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLIAM BLASSER OF THE LAW OFFICES OF PARVIZ DARABI, TEL. NO. (650) 343-5357 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. THEONA ZHORDANIA OF MCKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE LLP, TEL. NO. (213) 892-4915 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT INFINITY;

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT INFINITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled in all respect except the Plaintiff must identify the name of salesperson who made the misrepresentation or after reasonable effort, Plaintiff cannot determine his name. The court granted 30 days leave to amend the complaint. The fraud causes of action are pled with sufficient particularity. The specificity requirement is relaxed where the allegations show that the defendant likely possesses full information concerning the facts of the controversy. Paragraph 43 of the amended complaint alleges that the salesperson was defendant's employee and he can be identified by looking at employee records. Plaintiff pleads justifiable reliance and the fraud exception to the parole evidence rule may apply. The salesperson translated the documents for plaintiff and misrepresented the terms. Plainitff's allegations of concealment are proper even though a fiduciary relationship does not exist. Concealment can be established where there is partial disclosure. Request for Judicial Notice is denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order by way of e-mail) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT INFINITY INSURANCE COMPANY, A INDIANA CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off calendar. A First Amended Complaint was filed February 23, 2012. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
